notes: Bishop of Trier around 550 AD, known for opposing Arianism among Germanic peoples.

docs.title: Letter 6
docs.author: Nicetius of Trier
docs.subject_summary: Bishop Nicetius to the emperor on Arianism
docs.excerpt: … of Trier [in modern Germany], a forthright Frankish bishop, writes directly to the Emperor on the danger of Arianism [the heresy that held Christ was subordinate to, not equal with, God the Father], which had spread among the Germanic peoples.
